verses.text: God reminds the Jews in Egypt how Judah was made desolate by its idolatry. The lesson is plain. Past judgments are set before us that we might not repeat the sins that caused them.
God reminds the Jews in Egypt how Judah was made desolate by its idolatry. The lesson is plain. Past judgments are set before us that we might not repeat the sins that caused them.
God reminds the Jews in Egypt how Judah was made desolate by its idolatry. The lesson is plain. Past judgments are set before us that we might not repeat the sins that caused them.
He sent His prophets early and often, but they would not turn from burning incense to other gods. Patience was spurned. God's repeated warnings only deepen the guilt of those who refuse them.
He sent His prophets early and often, but they would not turn from burning incense to other gods. Patience was spurned. God's repeated warnings only deepen the guilt of those who refuse them.
He sent His prophets early and often, but they would not turn from burning incense to other gods. Patience was spurned. God's repeated warnings only deepen the guilt of those who refuse them.
Why commit this great evil against their own souls, He asks, provoking Him in the land of their refuge? Sin is self-destruction. To rebel against God is to cut oneself off from life.

He sets His face against them; the remnant in Egypt shall be consumed, scarcely any returning. Their refuge becomes their grave. To flee God's discipline into disobedience is to flee into death.

Defiantly they answer that they will keep burning incense to 'the queen of heaven' as before, for then they had plenty. They credit idols for past ease. A hardened heart twists even God's mercies into reasons for sin.

Jeremiah replies that it was this very idolatry the LORD remembered, and for it the land became desolate. They had the cause exactly backward. Sin, not the worship of God, is what ruined them.

Since they will keep their vows to the idol, let them, but God's word shall stand, and few shall escape. He gives them up to their choice. There is a point where God lets the obstinate have their way to their cost.

The sign: He will give Pharaoh-hophra to his enemies, as He gave Zedekiah. A pledge of judgment. God confirms His word by a coming event none can deny.
